name: The Siege Tombs
description: A dimension of sunbaked pyramids and crumbling war-monuments stretching across an endless dusty plateau. The sky burns a hazy rose-red, and the ruins are scarred by some ancient siege that never ended — war machines still smolder, and the soldiers entombed here have not stopped fighting. Everything is massive in scale: towering obelisks, half-collapsed colosseums, and siege ramps carved into cliff faces.
mood: Oppressive grandeur. Everything was built to last forever and is now crumbling. The scale makes you feel small, and the undead garrison makes you feel unwelcome.

enemyThemes.fodder: Tomb sentinels — skeletal soldiers with spears that patrol in rigid formations
enemyThemes.ranged: Siege throwers — undead artillerists that lob heavy projectiles in arcs
enemyThemes.melee: Entombed juggernauts — massive armored revenants that charge in straight lines
enemyThemes.elite: War chariots — spectral vehicle-rider pairs that trample across the battlefield
enemyThemes.boss: The Siege Pharaoh — an enormous undead commander atop a crumbling throne-platform, directing troops and raining down siege fire

mechanicalIdentity: Raw force and area denial. Enemies hit very hard in straight lines and zones, with heavy knockback and area-of-effect attacks. Less status effects, more about avoiding devastating telegraphed strikes. Juggernauts push through the battlefield like battering rams.

structureThemes.decorations: Broken obelisks, scattered siege ammunition, burial urns, cracked sarcophagi, war banners on tilted poles, sand drifts
structureThemes.walls: Pyramid stone blocks, siege barricades, toppled columns, sandstone ramparts, collapsed archways

itemThemes: Weapons of war pulled from the tombs — heavy bronze khopeshes, siege crossbows, tower shields emblazoned with forgotten dynasties, war drums that bolster allies, and sealed canopic jars that explode with cursed energy
